title: grindelia squarrosa var. integrifolia
intro: Grindelia squarrosa var. integrifolia, also known as Gumweed, is a plant species in the daisy family Asteraceae, characterized by its large, grayish leaves and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Grindelia squarrosa var. integrifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and acting as a natural expectorant to ease congestion. Grindelia squarrosa var. integrifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used to make a topical salve or poultice for skin issues.
